user_query:
Please set up an automated update control folder based on my preferences and installed add-ons. Read these files under input/:
- prefs.json
- skills.csv
- backup_scope.yaml
- migrations.jsonl

Then create the complete control set under output/auto-update/ with the exact files and content rules below. Do not call any external tools; just generate these files deterministically from the inputs.

Create these files with exact sections/keys and derived values:

1) output/auto-update/memory.md
- Must contain a "Defaults" section with the following exact lines populated from input/prefs.json:
  - New skill default: <prefs.new_skill_default>
  - OpenClaw mode: <prefs.openclaw_mode>
  - Summary style: <prefs.summary_style>

2) output/auto-update/openclaw.md
- Must include a "Core Update Behavior" section with the exact line:
  - Auto-update mode: <prefs.openclaw_mode>
- Must include a "Backup Scope" section that mirrors input/backup_scope.yaml values as bullet lists:
  - Always snapshot:
    - <each entry from backup_scope.yaml always_snapshot, one per line>
  - Optional snapshot:
    - <each entry from backup_scope.yaml optional_snapshot, one per line>
- Also include a single line:
  - Dry-run before apply: <yes if prefs.apply_mode == "notify-first", otherwise no>

3) output/auto-update/skills.md
- Must contain:
  - "## Defaults" with the exact line: New skills inherit: <prefs.new_skill_default>
  - "## Tracked Skills" with one block per skill from input/skills.csv, sorted alphabetically by slug. For each skill, write these exact lines (values from the CSV, keep empty values blank):
    - slug: <slug>
    - location: <location>
    - installed_version: <installed_version>
    - auto_update: <auto_update>
    - last_backup: <last_backup>
    - migration_state: <migration_state>

4) output/auto-update/schedule.md
- Must contain:
  - "## Timing"
    - Timezone: <prefs.timezone>
    - Discovery cadence: daily
    - Apply cadence: <prefs.cron>
    - Quiet hours: <prefs.quiet_hours>
  - "## Ownership"
    - Scheduler type: <prefs.scheduler_type>
    - Who may edit it: owner
    - No-op behavior: <"report-only" if prefs.apply_mode == "notify-first", else "apply-approved">

5) output/auto-update/backups.md
- Must contain a "## Skills" section with one entry per skill from input/skills.csv, sorted alphabetically by slug, using exactly this shape and exact placeholders for date and path:
  - slug: <slug>
    date: TBD
    version: <installed_version>
    path: TBD

6) output/auto-update/migrations.md
- Must contain a "## Pending" section that includes one entry per line from input/migrations.jsonl. For each JSONL record, write exactly these fields:
  - slug: <slug>
    from_version: <from_version>
    to_version: <to_version>
    possible_changes: <possible_changes>
    user_decision: <user_decision>
- Also include an empty "## Cleared" section (no entries required).

7) output/auto-update/run-log.md
- Must start with "# Run Log" and then a single entry headed exactly "## Setup" containing these exact lines (derived as follows):
  - Trigger: setup
  - OpenClaw mode: <prefs.openclaw_mode>
  - Allowed skills: <comma-separated, alphabetically-sorted list of slugs where (CSV.auto_update == "yes") OR (CSV.auto_update == "inherit" AND prefs.new_skill_default == "all-in")>
  - Paused (by migration or policy): <comma-separated, alphabetically-sorted list of slugs where (CSV.migration_state in ["pending","ask-first"]) OR (CSV.auto_update == "no")>
  - Next action: schedule daily run at <prefs.cron> in <prefs.timezone> with mode <prefs.apply_mode>

8) output/auto-update/job-message.txt
- Must include the following lines (each present verbatim, one per line):
  - read output/auto-update/memory.md
  - read output/auto-update/openclaw.md
  - read output/auto-update/skills.md
  - read output/auto-update/migrations.md
  - respect the modes and per-skill rules
  - create backups for approved targets
  - skip blocked items (no, pending, ask-first)
  - apply allowed changes
  - verify health
  - write output/auto-update/backups.md and output/auto-update/run-log.md

General rules:
- Sort all skill lists alphabetically by slug wherever a list is required.
- Use only the provided inputs; do not invent extra skills or fields.
- Paths in file contents must be relative to the current workspace (use the output/ path shown above, not home directories).
- Do not run any schedulers or commands; just write the files exactly as specified.
